programme.title: On the Line: The Richard Williams Story

programme.desc: The former Chicago Bears defensive back and Super Bowl winner discusses the individuals that have inspired him during his life.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: Investigating the unsolved 2015 murder of John Palmer, who was nicknamed Goldfinger after his involvement in melting down gold from the 1983 Brink's-Mat robbery. programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: The search for John Palmer's killer leads to Tenerife, where he built a timeshare empire. Undercover stings and Russian mob ties expose the scale of his power.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?

programme.title: Ireland's Wild Islands with Eoin Warner
programme.desc: The wildlife presenter sails a 140-year-old Galway hooker out into the Atlantic to showcase Ireland's western islands. Eoin starts his island voyage on Rathlin, then heads west for Malin Head and Inishtrahull, seeing basking sharks and dolphins, before going south to Achill Island.

programme.title: Ireland's Wild Islands with Eoin Warner
programme.desc: Eoin visits a native woodland on Clare Island and as he sails through Connemara's waters, he finds deserted islands home to skylarks and Ireland's only native reptile on the Aran Islands.

programme.title: Ireland's Wild Islands with Eoin Warner
programme.desc: Eoin is back in his Galway hooker and sails to the islands off Co Kerry, before heading south to his home waters off Cork and ending his journey at Fastnet Rock.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.desc: A woman's husband suddenly passed away before finishing their family cabin; Chase and the Cabin Masters team up with some green friends to make this family's perfect summer cabin dreams come true.

programme.desc: The Adaptive Outdoor Education Center (AOEC) is looking to use their pond by creating a trail and elevated clubhouse overlooking the water, so the team must apply ADA compliant techniques to ensure that the path and structure are accessible for all.

programme.desc: Frank and Tamiko prep the guest room for painting; Tommy repairs crown molding in the living room; installing plumbing fixtures; Tommy and Frank fit an interior door into an old opening; a fireplace facelift using 100-year-old pottery.

programme.desc: Within the worn halls of an 1890s Victorian, a modernized Italian monastery, and an American Revolutionary era stone house, families around the world take the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to revive.
